$comment: FC17.9(b) C23 <stdbit.h> (D-FULLC-STDBIT): runtime witness for ALL 14 type-generic bit operations (N3096 §7.18) across the 4 concrete widths (uc/us/ui/ull), the 4-way _Generic form, the per-format _ul macro, and the edge inputs (0, 1, all-ones, single high bit). Each stdc_* macro (from <stdbit.h>) expands to a __builtin_stdc_<op>_<T> compiler intrinsic that lowers to a width-correct, single-eval, BRANCHLESS composition of the 3 hardware bit-count primitives (Popcount/Clz/Ctz) + universal ALU verbs — NOT a linkable call. Fold-resistant: each op runs behind a wrapper fn taking the value as an arg, and the Clz/Ctz/Popcount cores are NOT in ConstFold's fold set — so the `release` (shippedPipeline) arm proves the ops survive Inlining + the full optimizer. Differential: x86-64 (native POPCNT/LZCNT/TZCNT) and arm64 (native CLZ + RBIT-then-CLZ ctz + SWAR popcount) MUST agree — a single-arch width/clz-ctz bug changes the sum. Exit derived BY HAND from C23 §7.18 (independently, not from DSS): 32+4+7+8+16+4+5+8+5+4+1+16+4+8 (the 14 ops) + 8+8+1+1+0+0+8 (edges/all-ones) + 8+8+4 (generic ×2 + per-format _ul) = 168.
